prompt: I want to build the port turn-up use case from the spec file.

expectations.evidence: Response includes 'mkdir -p port-turn-up' to create the use-case directory before forking the spec.

expectations.text: Forks spec to customer-spec.md (does not modify original in spec-files/)
expectations.evidence: Response copies spec-files/spec-port-turn-up.md to port-turn-up/customer-spec.md via cp. Explicitly states 'The original in spec-files/ stays untouched.'

expectations.text: Does NOT authenticate or pull platform data for spec path
expectations.evidence: No authentication, no curl calls, no bootstrap data pull. Response explicitly states 'No authentication or platform data pull happens at this stage -- that comes later, after you've approved the spec at Gate 1.' The skill's Path B section says 'No auth here. No data pulls. Just route.'

expectations.text: Tells engineer what happens next (understand, Gate 1, discover, design, Gate 2, build)
expectations.evidence: Response includes the full 6-step plan: Understand, Approve the spec (Gate 1), Discover, Design, Approve the design (Gate 2), Build. Ends with 'You own both gates. Nothing gets built without your sign-off.'

expectations.text: Hands off to /solution-design with only the forked spec file
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly shows the workspace contains exactly one file (customer-spec.md) and states handoff to /solution-design. No .auth.json, no openapi.json, no tasks.json -- only the forked spec.
